questions.question: Which Liquid expression groups posts by year for a year-grouped archive page?
questions.explanation: `group_by_exp` takes the item variable name and a Liquid expression evaluated per item. Formatting the date with `'%Y'` yields the year as the group key. `group_by` only accepts a property path, not an expression.

questions.question: Your blog has 60 posts and readers complain they can't find anything on the archive page. Which change addresses the root cause?
choices: Add `paginate: 10` to `_config.yml` so posts are split into pages
Group the archive by year, one section per year with one line per post
Hide the archive and link to the RSS feed instead
questions.explanation: Year-grouped archives let readers scan or Cmd-F in a single page. Pagination hides content behind clicks and makes scanning harder, not easier - exactly the wrong response for an archive a reader is searching.

questions.question: You want a featured post's summary on the homepage to be a hand-written sentence, not the post's opening paragraph. What's the cleanest way?
choices: Add an `excerpt:` key to the post's front matter and render `post.excerpt` on the homepage
Copy-paste the summary into the homepage template and hard-code it
Wrap the opening paragraph in a `<details>` so Jekyll uses the next paragraph
Set `excerpt_separator: "<!--summary-->"` at the top of the post body
questions.explanation: A front-matter `excerpt:` key overrides Jekyll's default and the separator. It keeps the summary with the post (versionable, reviewable) and works automatically wherever `post.excerpt` is rendered.
